name: Ferrari N.V.
summary: Ferrari N.V. (RACE, Consumer Discretionary) — composite stance NEUTRAL / WATCH. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $1.85B (+3.2% YoY). Valuation 50.0x forward P/E (+614% vs peers). Spot $332.21 (below 200-DMA).
bull: Stable growth profile — revenue at +3.2% YoY in FY2026 Q1 (TTM $17.92B). Not a hyper-growth story but the durability of the print — combined with the cash-flow profile — supports a value-with-modest-growth thesis.

Sell-side target points higher — consensus PT of $520 (vs spot $332.21) implies +56.5% upside. Cross-checked against the peer-median PE FY1 of 7.0x, the discount to fair value looks underwritten by both buy-side and sell-side.
bear: Trend rolling over — spot $332.21 sits -15.2% relative to the 200-DMA ($391.75). The break-down of long-term trend often precedes earnings disappointment by 1–2 quarters as smart-money distribution leads the fundamental print.

Valuation premium — 50.0x forward P/E vs peer median of 7.0x — a 614% premium. The setup requires continued out-execution to maintain; any reversion to peer-median multiples compresses the stock meaningfully even before any earnings revision.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 8% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 0% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $314.63 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line:
NEUTRAL / WATCH. Ferrari N.V. (RACE) printed $1.85B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+3.2% YoY). At 50.0x forward P/E, the stock carries a 614% premium to peers (7.0x median) — the multiple needs continued execution to defend.

Technical regime is impaired: spot $332.21 below the 200-DMA ($391.75, -15.2%) — long-term trend has rolled over. Sell-side consensus PT of $520 implies +56.5% upside. 30-day implied move is ±9.2%; 90-day is ±17.8%. Stay on the watchlist; wait for either a fundamental inflection (acceleration or margin expansion) or a valuation reset before initiating.
